exemplar.title: Ketogenic diet and its effect on athleteâs performance by observing changes in VO2 Max

detailedExemplarData.title: Ketogenic diet and its effect on athleteâs performance by observing changes in VO2 Max

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear and focused research question revisited throughout the report
Adequate background on ketosis, VOâ max, and HIIT providing context
weaknesses: Excessive digressions into obesity and leptin topics dilute focus
Methodology limited to only three secondary studies with conceptual and calculation errors
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Across the three strands, the investigation communicates the topic adequately (3â4 band), formulates a clear, narrowly focused RQ (5â6 band), and shows partially effective method selection (3â4 band). Averaging these performances yields a mid-band score of 4. This reflects adequate communication and focus with room to tighten explanations and expand methodological rigor.

strengths: Accurate and consistent use of key biological terminology (e.g., ketosis, RER)
Effective application of primary source data in tables and graphs
weaknesses: Occasional misinterpretation of statistical significance and missing effect-size discussion
Uneven citation practice with some assertions lacking precise references
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Source material is applied in a mostly relevant but not fully sophisticated manner, and terminology usage is generally accurate (upper half of 3â4 band). The score of 4 captures solid foundational knowledge with scope to deepen statistical interpretation and citation precision.

strengths: Basic quantitative analyses are linked explicitly to the RQ
Systematic evaluation tables list study limitations and future considerations
Logical progression from analysis through discussion and recommendations
weaknesses: Discussion evaluations are generic and conclusions only partially supported
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three critical-thinking strands sit at the lower half of the 4â6 band, yet the student demonstrates sufficient analytical linkage to the RQ, coherent evaluation tables, and a discernible argument. A score of 5 rewards these strengths while acknowledging errors and generic conclusions.

strengths: Clear overall structure with conventional EE chapters and consistent page numbering
Use of tables, figures, and references indicates familiarity with formatting conventions
weaknesses: Redundant headings and embedded appendices impede readability
Low-resolution images, inconsistent reference styling, and residual HTML artefacts remain
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets the basic expectations of the 3â4 band: structure is generally appropriate and layout conventions are applied, but flaws in navigation, graphics quality, and formatting consistency limit a higher score.

strandSelections.reasoning: The essay contains a contents page, numbered sections and conventional EE chapters, so the overall structure is appropriate.  However, some headings are redundant (âData researchâ then âData selectionâ), appendix material is embedded in the main body, and long blocks of text hamper readability.  Thus the structure is clear but not consistently well handled, meriting the lower score in the 3-4 band.

The work includes title, word count, page numbers, tables and figures with captions, and references in a consistent style.  Yet several in-text images are low resolution, some tables break awkwardly, and minor HTML artefacts (e.g. â<sup>â) persist.  These issues show that layout conventions are applied but not flawlessly, situating the essay at the lower end of the 3-4 band.

The essay follows a logical structure from introduction through literature review, data presentation, analysis and discussion, allowing a discernible line of argument.  Nonetheless, frequent digressions, occasional repetition and minor logical leaps (e.g. jumping from body-temperature changes to ATP output) create inconsistency.  The argument is clear enough to follow but lacks sustained coherence, fitting the lower half of the 4-6 band.

The student states the focusâ the influence of a ketogenic diet on VO2 max during 15-30 min HIITâ early in the title, the RQ and the introduction.  Background information on ketosis, VO2 max and HIIT is provided and is largely biologically correct, giving the reader enough context to understand why the question matters.  However, sections are lengthy and sometimes digress to general issues such as obesity or leptin that do not directly serve the investigation, so the explanation is not consistently concise or well targeted.  Overall the topic is communicated adequately with sufficient, but not always tightly linked, explanation, fitting the 3-4 band.

Relevant primary sources (Urbain 2017, Lukas 2018, Burke 2020) are cited and tabulated, and data are extracted into graphs and simple statistics, demonstrating appropriate but not sophisticated use.  However, reliance on secondary summaries, occasional misinterpretation of statistical significance, and inclusion of tangential medical facts limit depth.  Application is therefore mostly relevant but only partially effective, aligning with the 3-4 band.

The student performs basic quantitative analysisâpercentage change and paired t-testsâon each dataset and comments on statistical significance.  Links to the RQ are explicit, yet the analytical depth is modest: sample sizes of three are accepted without critique, some calculations are mathematically incorrect, and confounding variables are largely acknowledged rather than addressed.  This demonstrates adequate, partly relevant analysis consistent with the lower end of the 4-6 band.

Method is limited to secondary-data analysis of three published studies.  The student gives selection criteria (website domain, key words, participant health, professional authorship) and outlines basic statistical treatment (percentage change, paired t-test).  Nevertheless, only three papers are used, raw data are often missing, and calculations (e.g. â-130 % changeâ) reveal conceptual errors.  Control of confounding factors is acknowledged but cannot be implemented in a desk study.  Evidence of thoughtful but only partially effective selection places the work in the 3-4 band, at the lower end.

The discussion summarises findings from the three studies, compares them to the original hypothesis, and identifies methodological weaknesses and limitations in table form.  Evaluation is present but often generic (e.g. âincrease resources,â âconduct experiment in labâ), and conclusions about the keto dietâs limited effect are only partly supported by the flawed data.  Consequently, the work shows adequate but not robust evaluationâappropriate to 4-6 band, low level.

The research question is clearly worded (âWhat is the effect of switching to a high-fat low-carbohydrate (keto) diet on VO2 max during 15-30 min HIIT?â) and is revisited throughout the work.  It narrows the investigation to one dietary intervention, one definition of performance and one exercise duration, showing a reasonable degree of focus.  Some later discussion strays to endurance events and health claims, indicating minor dilution of focus, so the strand fits the lower end of the 5-6 band.

Key biological termsâketosis, ketone bodies, VO2 max, HIIT, RERâare generally defined and used appropriately throughout.  Minor lapses (e.g. âcarbohydrate oxidation which effects energy productionâ) and some typographical inconsistencies are present but do not impede meaning.  Overall usage is mostly accurate and consistent, meriting the upper half of the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: The sampling considerations section lists normative VO2 values but lacks critique on how these norms apply to study populations. Discuss representativeness and potential bias.
Several statistical calculations (e.g. â-130 % changeâ) appear erroneous. Revisit raw data and formula application to correct conceptual errors.
The formula for percentage change is presented correctly as $$\Delta\% = \frac{x_2 - x_1}{x_1} \times 100$$, showing sound understanding of quantitative methods.
Interpretation of paired t-test results is surface-level; degrees of freedom and effect sizes are omitted. Provide fuller statistical discussion to support conclusions.
The discussion acknowledges limitations across datasets, demonstrating critical evaluation; however, recommendations for addressing confounders could be more specific.
The argument leaps from VO2 findings to body temperature and ATP without explicit linking rationale. Strengthen logical flow by explaining biochemical connections.
Evaluation tables systematically list limitations and modifications, reflecting a strong capacity to critically appraise study design.

The research question is concisely framed (âWhat is the effect of switching to a high fat low carbohydrate (keto diet) on athletic performanceâ¦?â) and revisited throughout, demonstrating clear focus on one dietary intervention and specific performance measure.
Including the word count at the start meets the formatting requirements, showing attention to IB guidelines.
The contents page lists many sections without page numbers (e.g. âChoice of topicâ), which hampers navigation. Ensure all entries have accurate page references.
The introduction provides broad context on dieting trends but diverges into general obesity discussion. Focus more tightly on VO2 max and HIIT to maintain relevance.
Citations in the background are unevenly applied (e.g., keto benefits are asserted without specific study references). Integrate precise sources to support claims.
Terminology like ketosis and RER is defined accurately, demonstrating solid use of subject concepts. Continue this consistency throughout.
The literature review diverges into obesity etiology and leptin resistance, which is not directly linked to VO2 max or HIIT. Tighten this section to relevant athletic performance factors.
The relevance section appropriately links VO2 max to athletic performance, but it could explicitly connect how diet modulates VO2 in HIIT contexts to reinforce research focus.
The variables table is thorough for a primary experiment but exceeds scope for secondary-data analysis. Focus on data extraction methods rather than participant controls.
Ethical considerations are clearly described, demonstrating an understanding of research constraints and participant welfare for secondary data use.
Results from Urbain (2017) are clearly summarized and tabulated, showing appropriate application of source data.
Figure captions lack detailed axis labels and sample sizes, making interpretation difficult. Enhance figure annotations for clarity.
Future considerations are varied and address scope expansion well, demonstrating intellectual initiative and authenticity in planning next steps.
Reference list shows inconsistencies in formatting style and ordering (e.g., mixed date styles, missing italics). Standardize to a single citation format.
Reflections on researcher qualifications and resource constraints are descriptive rather than evaluative. Incorporate insights on decision processes to deepen reflection.
Reference list is extensive and draws from primary journal articles, demonstrating strong engagement with relevant literature.
One reference URL is truncated (âMayo Clinic. Retrieved May 5, 2023, from https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-â¦â). Ensure full URLs are present for retrievability.
Pages are consistently numbered and section headers are applied, supporting coherent structure despite minor redundancies.
Some in-text HTML artefacts (e.g. â<sup>â) remain and images appear low resolution. Clean up formatting and replace with high-quality graphics.
